title: TITAN pipeline parameters
description: Parameter schema for TITAN, a Nextflow DSL2 eukaryotic genome annotation pipeline.

output_dir.description: TITAN output directory. Required for production runs.

new_assembly.description: Target genome assembly to annotate, FASTA. Required for production runs.

previous_assembly.description: Reference assembly used by Liftoff, FASTA. Required for production runs.

previous_annotations.description: GFF3 annotation matching previous_assembly. Required for production runs.

RNAseq_samplesheet.description: CSV with sampleID,SRA_or_FASTQ,library_layout columns. Required for production runs.

RNAseq_data_dir.description: Directory containing local RNA-seq FASTQ/FASTA files referenced by sampleID.

protein_samplesheet.description: CSV with organism,filename protein evidence rows. Required for production runs.

egapx_paramfile.description: EGAPx YAML input file. Required for production runs.

publish_intermediates.description: Publish intermediate/debug outputs under output_dir/intermediate_files and output_dir/tmp.

egapx_cpus.description: CPU override for the TITAN EGAPx wrapper process.

egapx_revision.description: EGAPx runner Git tag or revision to fetch when egapx_runner_dir is not provided.

egapx_executor.description: Executor passed to the nested EGAPx runner.

egapx_data_version.description: EGAPx data version passed to the nested EGAPx runner.

egapx_runner_dir.description: Optional local EGAPx runner directory. False downloads the pinned revision.

egapx_local_cache_dir.description: Optional persistent EGAPx local cache directory.

ena_download_timeout_seconds.description: Per-request timeout for ENA/SRA FASTQ downloads.

ena_max_download_attempts.description: Maximum ENA/SRA download attempts per file.

ena_retry_wait_seconds.description: Wait time between ENA/SRA download retries.

ena_process_max_retries.description: Maximum Nextflow process retries for ENA/SRA preparation tasks.

ena_verify_md5.description: Verify ENA MD5 checksums when metadata provides them.
